4, [} AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES
Defendant alleges the following additional reasons that plaintiff is not entitled to recover anything:
1.Statute of Limitations as to all causes of acticn.
2.Hez Conduct is not actionable as to all causes of action because it was protected
activity uncer the Fair Employment Housing Act.
3.Cross-Complainant fails to state a cause of action for any cf its allegations or
causes of action.
4.The Crime Fraud defense because USF sought to cover up its illegat conduct (all
causes of action}.
5.Post-Termination Conduct cannot serve as the basis for a complaint for breach of
contract secking ‘njuctive or declaratcry relief and it is only that conduct that
alleged-y breached Cross-Complaintant's policies or agreements.
6.The doctrine of equitable estoppel applies to all causes of action.
7.Cross-complainant lacks standing to assert a cause of action relating to third party
privacy rights in the inten. tort, conversion and declaratory relie! causes of action.
8.The Cross-Complaint is void as violating public policy with respect to all causes of
action. |
9.The Cross-Claim is barred in its entirety as USF has already soucht relief by
asserting the after acquired evidence doctrine and therefore seeks doucle recovery.
1G.Statute of frauds as to the breach of cortract causes of action for intunctive and
declaratory relief.
11.The purpose of the contract was illegal in that it sought to prevent Ms.
Westerfield from establishing pretext and improper motive in her discrimination case.
12.The information that is allegedly confidenzial is not confidential and therefore
no basis exists for the cross-complaint in its entirety.
(KJ Continued on Attachment 4.
5. C) Other:
Cross-Defendant states that she discovered illegal conduct by defendant in
chat it reported false numbers to the US Government. She complained about
this and the false reasons for the elimination of her position. Shortiy
after her complaint, she was fired. The information that Cross-Complainant
seeks to have returned is necessary to establish the illegai conduct and to
establish Cross-Complainant's pretextual, retaliatory termination of her
and of its discriminatory animus. Therefore, her conduct cannot serve as
the basis for any cross-complaint against her. Moreover, she never agreed
to keep information about the number and types of students with
disabilities confidential as this information is not confidential and must
be reported to the federal government.
6. DEFENDANT PRAYS
a. that plaintiff take nothing.
b. for costs of suit.
c. Ki) other (specify):
1.Reasonable Attorney's Fees
2.Such other relief as the Court may deem proper.
3.Indemnity for defense and damages resulting from this
cross-action.

Attachment 4 to Patricia Westerfield’s Answer to Second Amended Cross Complaint |
13" Affirmative Defense: Indemnity (all causes of action)

13. Because Cross-complainant complains about acts that were carried out during her time as
an employee, see seeks indemnity under the California Labor Code for the costs of
defending against those acts.

14" Affirmative Defense: Failure to Exhaust Administrative Remedies (all causes of action)

14. Because Cross-complainant complains about acts that allegedly were carried out during -
her time as an employee, sec secks indemnity under the California Labor Code for the
costs of defending against those acts.

: 15" Affirmative Defense: Unclean Hands (all causes of action)
15. | The Complaint is barred by the doctrine of unclean hands. |
16" Affirmative Defense: Waiver (all causes of action) ,
16. | The Complaint is barred by the doctrine of waiver.
17" Affirmative Defense: Failure to Exhaust Administrative Remedies (First, Second &
; Fourth Causes of Action)

17. The First, Second and Fourth Causes of action are barred in that Cross-Complainant has
failed to exhaust its administrative remedies under employer grievance procedures under
the alleged contract (personnel manual) and/or failed to petition the court for mandamus
with respect to the first and second causes of action and Cross-Complainant failed to
exhaust statutory administrative remedies as to its claims under the Califomia Labor Code

as to the fourth cause of action.
' Westerfield v. USF Case No. CGC 03-417577 Answer to Cross-Claim

 
Attachment 4 to Patricia Westerfield’s Answer ta Second Amended Cross Complaint
18" Affirmative Defense: Justified Conduct (All Causes of Action)

18. The Complaint is barred in that Cross-Complainant seeks damages for conduct that
complied with its general polices and procedures to pursue the truth and expose fraud and
deception by any of its employees which is what Ms. Westerfield attempted to do with
respect to USF's false justifications for her termination and its false reporting of students |
with disabilities.

19" Affirmative Defense: Set Off (All Causes of Action)
19, In the event that Cross-Complainant recovers damages, such damages should be set off
against the amount of damages Ms. Westerfield is precluded from recovering under the
After Acquired Evidence Doctrine.
| 20 Affirmative Defense: Lack of Damages (A! Causes of Action)
| 20: The Complaint is barred in that Cross-Complainant has failed to suffer any quantifiable
damages based on Ms. Westerfield’s conduct for which USF may retover. |
21* Affirmative Defense: Laches (all causes of action)

21. The Complaint is barred by the doctrine of laches.
